sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच | अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे | गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिता: ||
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṃ prajñā-vādāṃś ca bhāṣase | gatāsūn agatāsūṃś ca nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: You are grieving for what is not to be grieved over, and yet you speak words of wisdom; the wise do not lament for the living or the dead.
contextual_meaning: Krishna confronts Arjuna’s intellectual defense of his despair, exposing the split between his rational claims ('I speak as a wise man') and his emotional collapse. The verse marks the first therapeutic intervention: naming the incongruence between self-image and actual inner state.
psychological_state: Cognitive-dissonance crisis: Arjuna experiences himself as both 'learned' and helpless, revealing an unstable self-concept under existential threat.
trigger_scenario: A respected professional who lectures others on resilience suddenly freezes when receiving a terminal diagnosis, realizing their theories never touched their own mortality terror.
anchor_text: Feel the pain, but don’t call it wisdom until it matches your calm.
